checks.finding: The design decision to preserve the existing consent contract while changing the Python source boundary is justified by the injection risk and the no-contract-change constraint.
The specification has no open questions.
The Python and PowerShell runtime assumptions are bounded to existing repository-supported execution paths.

REQ-005 makes security-spec.md the canonical description of the PowerShell .NET HMACSHA256 byte-array safety difference, and the design delegates that detail there. The reviewed security specification did not yet state the byte-array conversion or prohibition on source interpolation, leaving AC-005 unsupported.
No performance requirement applies to this local, constant-size consent check.
The change is local to existing scripts and focused tests; it introduces no deployment topology or dependency change.
No persisted data or migration is involved.
The shell and PowerShell consent paths and their focused test suites are identified, with no external runtime integration.
The plan corrects the source-construction boundary and adds regression coverage without expanding the consent contract.
TEST-001 through TEST-007 name concrete shell and PowerShell test targets for acceptance, tamper denial, invalid conditions, hostile operands, and isolation.
No persisted business-domain model is introduced or changed.
